В соответствии с официальной документацией (http://docs.oracle.com/cd/E16655_01/win.121/e17732/featOraCommand.htm#ODPNT258) типу TimeSpan соответствует тип IntervalDS который задается посредством типа DbType.Object, который можно задать в linq2db с помощью DataType.Undefined.

title: Startup performance optimization
body_excerpt:
Hi,

I encountered a strange startup delay with linq2db for some type of queres. If run first

```
var u = ctx.Users.OrderBy(a=>a.Email).FirstOrDefault(); 
```

would take 1100 ms and 

```
var u = ctx.Users.FirstOrDefault();
```

would take only 300ms.

Profiling showed that the difference is caused by compiling members dictionary for mapping complex expressions such as string operations and convert functions, sql functions. These functions are not used in most queries, so I added few changes t…

title: Added UseQueryText feature
summary: [closed] Use plain text query instead of using command parameters. For enabling this option you have to set the dbManager.UseQueryText property to TRUE.
body_excerpt:
Use plain text query instead of using command parameters. For enabling this option you have to set the dbManager.UseQueryText property to TRUE.
After one query execution you can check the generated sql query using the property dbManager.LastQuery.

Why we need this? In Oracle, when using table partitions, the execution plan doesnt take in account a partition when using command parameters.

title: fix for GetDefaultMappingFromEnumType
summary: [merged] changed type.IsNullable() to enumType.IsNullable(). The issue I got was trying to update nullable int table field from the class having nullable Enum property. It appears that ConvertBuilder.GetDefaultMappingFromEnumType do not respect original enum type when trying to decide if ...
body_excerpt:
changed type.IsNullable() to enumType.IsNullable(). The issue I got was trying to update nullable int table field from the class having nullable Enum property. It appears that ConvertBuilder.GetDefaultMappingFromEnumType do not respect original enum type when trying to decide if the type mapping to should also be be made nullable. Please review and let me know if it makes sense for you.

Thanks!

title: Firebird sql optimizer fix
summary: [merged] Сори, что по русски пишу, но по английски я это описалово не осилю.
body_excerpt:
Сори, что по русски пишу, но по английски я это описалово не осилю.

Проблема:
Для  запросов типа такого:
db.GetTable<MyTable>().Any(x => x.Field == param);

должен генерится такой sql

```
select case
  when exists(exists(select * from mytable t where t.Field = @param)) then 1
  else 0
end from rdb$database
```

проблема в том, что Firebird sql optimizer инлайнит параметр. Для строк/чисел - это неважно, но вот если мы хотим искать по блобу, то тут возникает проблема, блобы же не могут быть лите…

title: PostgreSQL: quote database and schema names
body_excerpt:
Hello!
I've encountered a bug when using CamelCase schema name in PostgreSQL. While linq2db quotes table names if they contain upper case letters, but linq2db didn't quote database and schema names.
I provide a simple change for that problem and it helped in my case.

title: Use Ticks instead of time fields for greater precision
summary: [merged] This solves my problem with storing timetstamptz  - when using datetimediff as parameter it is converted using 6 decimal places to parameter (length attribute is ignored) and checking for equality is not possible. Now using timestamptz(6) we can check for equality cause last 3 pl...
body_excerpt: This solves my problem with storing timetstamptz  - when using datetimediff as parameter it is converted using 6 decimal places to parameter (length attribute is ignored) and checking for equality is not possible. Now using timestamptz(6) we can check for equality cause last 3 places are not rounded/cut off. Haven't noticed any side effects.

title: Fixed problem with generic filter predicates
summary: [merged] Hi! We have a problem with some complex queries. I think that the cause in line
body_excerpt:
Hi! We have a problem with some complex queries. I think that the cause in line 

```
if (me.Member == binding.Member)
```

When compared "me.Member" and "binding.Member" the first one can be interface preperty that is implemented by type with the second one. In this case comparision gives us "false" and then exception raised, but I think that it is possible to proceed.

Simplest solution is to replace "me.Member == binding.Member" with "me.Member.Name == binding.Member.Name", but it is not good…

commands. This pull request adds the missing extension method to call it
directly via a `DataConnection` object.

title: Rebind command return parameters
summary: [closed] If a command requires a parameter with `ParameterDirection.ReturnValue`,
body_excerpt:
If a command requires a parameter with `ParameterDirection.ReturnValue`,
its value must also be rebound after execution.

Otherwise e.g. calling an Oracle PL/SQL stored procedure that returns a value can be called, but the result cannot be obtained.

title: Adding support for fsharp climutable attribute
body_excerpt:
Hi.

To make Entity Framework work with F# records, you have to mark the records with the CLIMutableAttribute. This makes the porperties settable from other CLR languages, but still read-only from F#. It works pretty well.

I have a bunch of EF models that I'm trying to migrate to linq2db, but ironically linq2dbs support for records made this problematic for two reasons:

1. Linq2db exprect that records have only 1 constructor, but the CLIMutable attribute causes the record class to also have a …

title: Adding F# sharp record support for relational properties
body_excerpt:
Hi again

So this is my first attempt at proper support for relational properties in F# records.

Initially I played with copying the LoadWith logic from BuildDefaultConstructor and setting the association property mutable. That worked, but was obviously undesireable.

What was really needed was a way to pass the associated instance object to the record constructor. This PR is the result of that change.

title: fix CHAR(X)/NCHAR(X) padding trimming
summary: [merged] This PR addresses issue with padding trimming when we read data into char or string field. Current implementation uses string.TrimEnd, which trims about 20 different characters, but we need to trim only space (0x20).
body_excerpt:
This PR addresses issue with padding trimming when we read data into char or string field. Current implementation uses string.TrimEnd, which trims about 20 different characters, but we need to trim only space (0x20).
For mapping to char field current implementation uses same trimming logic as for mapping to string which could result in empty string if database returns trimmable character. It results in '\0' character in mapped field.

- LinqService trimming tests disabled as th…

Looks like CanCombineParameters flag was never supported for merge-based InsertOrUpdate query and we didn't spotted it before due to:

- Sybase doesn't use merge-based query for InsertOrUpdate
- for iSeries it wasn't spotted before due to a bug with duplicate parameters, which I fixed here https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/pull/686#pullrequestreview-40652778

title: Added Sql.NoConvert function.
body_excerpt:
Added Sql.NoConvert function. Sample usage:

```cs
from od in db.OrderDetail
select Sql.NoConvert(od.UnitPrice * od.Quantity * (decimal)(1 - od.Discount))
```
Before:
```sql
SELECT
    ([t1].[UnitPrice] * Convert(Decimal(29,10), [t1].[Quantity])) * Convert(Decimal(29,10), 1 - [t1].[Discount]) as [c1]
FROM
    [Order Details] [t1]
```
After:
```sql
SELECT
    ([t1].[UnitPrice] * [t1].[Quantity]) * (1 - [t1].[Discount]) as [c1]
FROM
    [Order Details] [t1]
```

title: fix execute cmd outside of script folder
body_excerpt:
According to documentation:
[https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/blob/538a1f2a2c632f3a56206559d1f5dad5a189c284/CONTRIBUTING.md#building](url)
Two batch files can be run to build the project but if Build.cmd or Compiled.cmd are executed not from within script folder, there is error
```
C:\Git\linq2db>.\Source\LinqToDB\Compile.cmd
MSBUILD : error MSB1009: Project file does not exist.
Switch: linq2db.sln
```

title: Added SqlProviderFlags.CustomFlags
summary: [closed] The DB2 iSeries provider has options as to how it handles Guids as they are not available natively in DB2 (handle them as a string or a blob).  This setting needs to be available in the SqlBuilder.  I used a constructor overload to set this value when creating the SqlBuilder in t...
body_excerpt:
The DB2 iSeries provider has options as to how it handles Guids as they are not available natively in DB2 (handle them as a string or a blob).  This setting needs to be available in the SqlBuilder.  I used a constructor overload to set this value when creating the SqlBuilder in the provider but the LinqService directly instantiates the SqlBuilder therefore in the LinqService the option is not available.

This change simply adds a CustomFlags list into  SqlProviderFlags allowing me to set my cust…

title: MERGE target hints support
body_excerpt:
Fix #1272

SQL Server supports hints on target table, this PR adds support for hints generation.

~Because merge uses different ordering of hint and table alias, you cannot use `With` method, which will put hint after alias and need to use `WithTableExpression` method to specify proper ordering:~
```cs
// {0} is a table
// {1} is an alias
// for merge target alias must go after hint
table.WithTableExpression("{0} WITH (HOLDLOCK) {1}")
```

title: Introduced expanding expression. Fixed problem with local function evaluation.
summary: [merged] For now developer can use expression tree variable inside LINQ Query.
body_excerpt:
For now developer can use expression tree variable inside LINQ Query.
Idea was borrowed from LinqKit library.

For example if you have variable
```cs
Expression<Func<SampleClass, bool>> predicate = c => c.Value > 1;
```
It is difficult to use it in linq. For now you can use it in the following way (using `Compile()`):
```cs
var query = from t in table
	from t2 in table.Where(predicate.Compile())
	select t;
```
Or compile with invokation:
```cs
var query = from t in table
	where predicate.Compile…

title: Use inline function parameters for SQL CE, for issue #1350
summary: [merged] I've created this pull request in relation to the following issue.
body_excerpt:
I've created this pull request in relation to the following issue.

https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/issues/1350

SQL CE 3.0 doesn't support parameters as function arguments, so I've forced them to be used inline.

I'm not sure if I've done this in the correct place, or in line with the development practices that the maintenance team use, so any feedback will be welcome.  I'm happy to make any changes you guys would like.

Thanks,

Matt

title: Fix postgresql provider "character varying" type bug
body_excerpt:
`_npgsqlTypeMap` don't contains `varchar varying` type. 
So I've replace it by `character varying`, that exists [there](https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/blob/master/Source/LinqToDB/DataProvider/PostgreSQL/PostgreSQLDataProvider.cs#L155) (and [in PostgreSQL docs](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/11/datatype-character.html)). Also I've added some additional check (for `character varying` with specified length).

title: [WIP] Implemented flexible mechanism for supporting custom types.
summary: [merged] Originally i have encountered problem when tried to use custom range types with `tstzrange`. Threre is only one type which implements timestamp range `NpgsqlRange<DateTime>` in Npgsql and there is no way to tell current engine that this particular convertation should be `timestam...
body_excerpt:
Originally i have encountered problem when tried to use custom range types with `tstzrange`. Threre is only one type which implements timestamp range `NpgsqlRange<DateTime>` in Npgsql and there is no way to tell current engine that this particular convertation should be `timestam with range`. Analysis showed that we have to improve mapping logic.

This PR also help in the following case: #1219
- [x] Fix tests

title: Implemented Associations from custom query.
summary: [merged] This feature allows to write associations from custom linq query.

This feature allows to write associations from custom linq query.
For example the following Associations are activated with this feature:

```cs
[Table]
public class SomeEntity
{
   [Column]
   public int Id { get; set; }

   [Column]
   public string OwnerStr { get; set; }

   [Association(QueryExpressionMethod = nameof(OtherImpl), CanBeNull = true)]
   public SomeOtherEntity Other { get; set; }

   [Association(QueryExpressionMethod = nameof(OtherImpl), CanBeNull = true)]
   public List<SomeOt…

title: [WIP] Dynamic columns
summary: [merged] Our project has multitenant model in which customers can add columns to existing entities, or create completly new entities. These can then be used through the application in the same way as built-in entities/columns. Also, most of the core functionalities are trully generic/dyna...
body_excerpt:
## Background

Our project has multitenant model in which customers can add columns to existing entities, or create completly new entities. These can then be used through the application in the same way as built-in entities/columns. Also, most of the core functionalities are trully generic/dynamic (like listings, forms, etc.). We have implemented custom LINQ provider very similar in design to linq2db, which supports our core scenarios, but is very limited in terms of extensibility and query tran…

summary: [merged] We store custom parameter setters in static variable,
body_excerpt:
We store custom parameter setters in static variable,

Having them as static doesn't give us anything, because each new instance of provider instance always overrides them.

From other side having them static leads to issues when different providers used for same database. E.g. we already removed statics for this reason from sybase provider. Now it gives issues when we run tests with both npgsql3/npgsql4 providers.

This PR is only for review. Functionality will be included into #1562

title: Extension point adds possibility to change implementation of DbCommand methods ExecuteScalar, ExecuteNonQuery, ExecureReader
summary: [merged] Extension point adds possibility to change implementation of `DbCommand` methods `ExecuteScalar`, `ExecuteNonQuery`, `ExecureReader` and their Async equivalents.
body_excerpt:
Extension point adds possibility to change implementation of `DbCommand` methods `ExecuteScalar`, `ExecuteNonQuery`, `ExecureReader` and their Async equivalents.

One of possible use cases: put commands into queue and initiate them in special separate thread, to overcome lock contention in `TimerQueue.Timer`

See: https://github.com/avtc/timer.noLock

Sometimes it is necessary to be able to compare classes of business objects.
I propose one of the ways to implement this functionality.

At first there was an idea to implement only two overloaded methods: `Equals` and `GetHashCode`. To do this, I would have to go through all the properties of each class.

But then I discovered the `СomparerBuilder` class with its method `GetEqualityComparer`, which already does all the work.

If there are any comments or suggestions for improving the implementa…

title: Improving Subquery optimization.
body_excerpt:
Added new IQueryable extensions.

- `AsSubQuery()` - forces Optimizer do not remove subquery during optimization.
- `HasUniqueKey()` - registers unique keys for tables or even subqueries. It should cause more aggressive join optimization. For example it is useful if we have `LEFT JOIN` to subquery and we know that result of this subquery always distinct by specific keys and in join we have used all of these keys. If in projection we do not use columns of this subquery - subquery will be removed …

title: Feature multi result set support
summary: [closed] Feature adds support for stored procedures that return multiple result sets.
body_excerpt:
Feature adds support for stored procedures that return multiple result sets.
This is important to support in some cases due to the inefficiency of making multiple queries (especially when ids need to be passed to subsequent calls).
As far as I can tell, there is no other way to achieve this using linq2db.

**Attribute approach**

A stored procedure is capable of returning multiple results sets in a given order.
Using attributes we can specify these indexes on a result object like so:

```cs
    …

This fixes the following issues with testing for SQL Server 2000:
* `sys.sp_addextendedproperty` is named differently in SQL Server 2000 with different parameters.

title: Fix SQL Server optimizations for TOP/OFFSET/FETCH
body_excerpt:
Fixes #2282 

This fix performs different checks for each version of SQL Server to match the supported features.

SQL Server 2000:
* Skip/Take not supported for updates
* Skip not supported at all (either via ROW_NUMBER or via OFFSET) - handled by SqlProviderFlags
* Only constants are allowed in TOP parameters - handled by SqlProviderFlags

SQL Server 2005/2008:
* Skip supported via ROW_NUMBER

SQL Server 2012/2017:
* Skip supported via OFFSET, but requires ORDER BY

This fix adds a parameter to…

title: Skip on Select All
body_excerpt:
Implemented the `ColumnAttribute.SkipOnSelectAll` property.
This mapping property can be applied to columns that should not be fetched by default.
Main use case are tables with a long set of columns and in which some of them may impact performance if retrieved by default (e.g.: Blob / Clob / XMLs / etc). In these cases, such columns must be arbitrarily selected in Linq queries to be fetched if the SkipOnSelectAll = true.

title: CreateTempTable cancellation token support and failure cleanup
body_excerpt:
This PR does the following:
1. Adds cancellation tokens to all existing asynchronous CreateTempTable overloads, passing it both to the CreateTable function, and also to the optional `Copy` (bulk import) or `Insert` (from another table) operation.
2. If an exception occurs during the `Copy` / `Insert` phase, the temporary table is dropped from the database.  If an error occurs during the drop, it is ignored; the original exception is still thrown back onto the stack.  Applies to both synchronous …

title: Fix SystemType on column descriptor when falling back to underlying d…
summary: [merged] When building the columns in EntityDescriptor, the implementation tryies to identify the column datatype from the systemtype and falls back to the underlying datatype to handle enums and nullables. However, when GetDataType finds no matching type it returns the SqlDataType.Undefi...
body_excerpt:
When building the columns in EntityDescriptor, the implementation tryies to identify the column datatype from the systemtype and falls back to the underlying datatype to handle enums and nullables. However, when GetDataType finds no matching type it returns the SqlDataType.Undefined constant, which has an object systemtype, so when falling back to underlying datatype the systemtype is always System.Object. I think MemberType was supposed to go there.

@MaceWindu this is the second patch needed f…

title: Allow different type of predicate when converting FuncLike in optimizer
summary: [merged] All the predicate type conversion cases in ConvertVisitor allow a different type to be returned from the conversion implemented by the provider. FuncLike predicate does not follow this and attempts to cast the converted inner function expression to an SqlFunction. This of course ...
body_excerpt:
All the predicate type conversion cases in ConvertVisitor allow a different type to be returned from the conversion implemented by the provider. FuncLike predicate does not follow this and attempts to cast the converted inner function expression to an SqlFunction. This of course will throw a cast exception when trying to replace the function expression with another valid expression.

This came up because the i Series has limitations on EXISTS predicates, so the EXISTS pseudo function needs to be…

title: Moved SQL comment generation to SqlBuilder
summary: [closed] Query runner built commands add SQL comments on the top, including provider details and any parameters.
body_excerpt:
Query runner built commands add SQL comments on the top, including provider details and any parameters. 

This breaks providers like the DB2i OleDb provider that don't support comments, or perhaps a provider that has different notation than double dash.

I moved the actual printing of the comments to the SQL Builder, so that each provider can override its behaviour.

This is part one of DB2i OleDb provider support.

title: Issue 2168 - Modify T4 name conflict detection for data types to include table names instead of only column names
body_excerpt:
Fixes #2168 

The issue is that `DataType` references in attributes would sometimes create ambiguous reference errors if there is a table named `DataType`. This ambiguous reference is already detected and prevented for field names; this fix expands that detection to include table names as well. When the condition is detected, `DataType` is fully qualified to `LinqToDB.DataType`.

summary: [merged] Allows for a connecionstring enclosed in brackets to be translated as a reference to another connectionstring name. Due to the architecture of providers, a schema or non database related option has to spawn a different provider, so you might be able to reuse the same connection s...
body_excerpt:
Allows for a connecionstring enclosed in brackets to be translated as a reference to another connectionstring name. Due to the architecture of providers, a schema or non database related option has to spawn a different provider, so you might be able to reuse the same connection string to test different providers.

This is PR No.1 of a series related to help porting tests to 3rd party providers (like IBM i).

title: [Tests] multischema fix
summary: [merged] Some providers (like the IBM i ) support loading multiple schemas (libraries in IBM i) so the schema providers can load tables with the same name. This patch handles schema testing so that it looks for tables in the default schema, and fallsback to schema ignorant search in case ...
body_excerpt:
Some providers (like the IBM i ) support loading multiple schemas (libraries in IBM i) so the schema providers can load tables with the same name. This patch handles schema testing so that it looks for tables in the default schema, and fallsback to schema ignorant search in case the providers doesn't support default schema information.

This is PR No.2 of a series related to help porting tests to 3rd party providers (like IBM i).

title: [Tests] Stripped all SQL trace comments from tests before execution
summary: [merged] A couple of tests stripped comments Access related comments before executing the SQL produced by the tracing mechanism when calling ToString on a query expression. To support more providers that can't handle inline comments in SQL, all comments were removed in said tests before t...
body_excerpt:
A couple of tests stripped comments Access related comments before executing the SQL produced by the tracing mechanism when calling ToString on a query expression. To support more providers that can't handle inline comments in SQL, all comments were removed in said tests before the raw SQL is dispatched for execution.

This is PR No.3 of a series related to help porting tests to 3rd party providers (like IBM i).

title: [Tests] Added sttribute to control Upsert tests on unsupported providers.
summary: [merged] The default behaviour from the base DataProvider for InsertOrUpdate is to throw an unsupported exception. All of the embedded linq2db providers support it so there is no ability to skip those tests. This patch adds an attribute to control this for tests tha invoke the InsertOrUpd...
body_excerpt:
The default behaviour from the base DataProvider for InsertOrUpdate is to throw an unsupported exception. All of the embedded linq2db providers support it so there is no ability to skip those tests. This patch adds an attribute to control this for tests tha invoke the InsertOrUpdate / InsertOrReplace functions, similarly to Merge. The list of unsupported providers is empty by default.

This is PR No.5 of a series related to help porting tests to 3rd party providers (like IBM i).

title: [Tests] Customization interceptor
summary: [merged] This patch helps control which tests will run without editing the source code. It is critical for porting and maintaining the test code base for 3rd party providers.
body_excerpt:
This patch helps control which tests will run without editing the source code. It is critical for porting and maintaining the test code base for 3rd party providers.

It basicly providers a class with 3 overridable methods that by default do nothing, and a static class that provides a singleton instance of that class. If someone wants to port the tests to their own project they only have to:

1. Create a new subclass of the interceptor class
2. Switch the singleton instance of the default implem…

title: [MiniProvider] Fix datareader expression resolve for wrapped provider
Optimize mapper expression to be compatible with SequentialAccess command behavior
body_excerpt:
Fix #2116
Fix #1185

Following providers support SequentialAccess behavior:
- Access (OleDb)
- MySql.Data
- npgsql
- System.Data.SqlClient
- Microsoft.Data.SqlClient
- SqlCe

---

To switch linq2db mapper to `SequentialAccess`-compatible mode user should set `LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.OptimizeForSequentialAccess` option to `true`.
It is disabled by default as it is possible to configure mappings that we cannot optimize, so we don't want to introduce breaking change.

title: Fix. Returning wrong identity at SapHana
body_excerpt:
Issue. SapHana.
With a large number of parallel insertWithIdentity into the same table, sometimes returned wrong identity. 
SELECT MAX(id) FROM table is not best way to get last identity at SapHana. Using CURRENT_IDENTITY_VALUE() is right way.

https://help.sap.com/viewer/7c78579ce9b14a669c1f3295b0d8ca16/Cloud/en-US/01e1a30aa542448da56da76233fea779.html

title: Lower allocations to improve ConvertBinaryToSql performance
summary: [merged] When profiling some benchmarks I noticed that there was a lot of traffic around `.ConvertBinaryToSql` (especially in bulk insert scenarios).
body_excerpt:
When profiling some benchmarks I noticed that there was a lot of traffic around `.ConvertBinaryToSql` (especially in bulk insert scenarios). 

I did some research and adapted [this](https://stackoverflow.com/a/24343727/2937845) into something that worked with StringBuilder.

Doing 'before/after' tests with this change show a pretty big improvement on paths where `.ConvertBinaryToSql` is called (with payloads in the 512b-1kb range, as much as 30% in a library benchmark against fast local IO)

title: SQL translation for string.IsNullOrWhitespace
body_excerpt:
Fix #2816

Databases with limited support:
- Access: only whitepace characters recognized by Access `*trim` functions will be considered as whitespace (0x20 (SPACE), 0x1680 (OGHAM SPACE MARK), 0x205F (MEDIUM MATHEMATICAL SPACE), 0x3000 (IDEOGRAPHIC SPACE)). Proper implementation requires 24 nested `Replace` calls and doesn't work anyway without [sandbox mode](https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/turn-sandbox-mode-on-or-off-to-disable-macros-8cc7bad8-38c2-4a7a-a604-43e9a7bbc4fb)

	.Distinct()
	.ToArray();
```
```sql
SELECT DISTINCT
	[t1].[DuplicateData]
FROM
	[OrderByDistinctData] [t1]
ORDER BY
	[t1].[OrderData1]
```

title: Use Oracle date/timestamp literals instead of functions
summary: [merged] Oracle supports ANSI SQL dates literal, as well as timestamp literals, which are shorter and more readable then calling parsing functions:
body_excerpt:
Oracle supports ANSI SQL dates literal, as well as timestamp literals, which are shorter and more readable then calling parsing functions:
- `DATE '2020-01-01'`
- `TIMESTAMP '2020-01-01 10:30:00.1234567 +00:00'`

Sadly, there's no such literal for dates with times, so this has to stay as `TO_DATE('2020-01-01 10:00:00', 'YYYY-MON-DD HH24:MIN-SS')`

title: Improve thread-safety
summary: [merged] The "Publish a ref atomically" pattern is safe only with a memory half-fence that prevents writes to be reordered after the publishing write (aka _Release Write_).
body_excerpt:
The "Publish a ref atomically" pattern is safe only with a memory half-fence that prevents writes to be reordered after the publishing write (aka _Release Write_).

Otherwise compiler and hardware are free to reorder writes as long as it isn't noticeable on a single thread, which can lead to other threads seeing the published reference pointing to a partially initialized object.

Fixes #2940

title: Add Sql.Default<T>()
summary: [merged] This PR adds an extension to use the SQL `DEFAULT` keyword.
body_excerpt:
This PR adds an extension to use the SQL `DEFAULT` keyword.
This keyword can be used as an insert value, for example:
```csharp
db.T.Insert(() => new T 
{
  Id = Sql.Default<int>(),
  Name = "Joe",
});
```
Generates:
```sql
insert into T (id, name)
values (default, "Joe")
```
Default is useful when you need to explicitly insert a non-null value but the DB decides what the value actually is, e.g.:
- Identities, sequences;
- Default constraints;
- Triggers.

title: Feat: IsDistinctFrom
summary: [merged] This PR adds `Sql.IsDistinctFrom` and `Sql.IsNotDistinctFrom`, which are intended to map to the standard `IS [NOT] DISTINCT FROM`, which is basically `=` but supports `NULL` and always returns `TRUE` or `FALSE`, never `UNKNOWN`.
body_excerpt:
This PR adds `Sql.IsDistinctFrom` and `Sql.IsNotDistinctFrom`, which are intended to map to the standard `IS [NOT] DISTINCT FROM`, which is basically `=` but supports `NULL` and always returns `TRUE` or `FALSE`, never `UNKNOWN`.

This is a useful function to have at hand, esp. since the equivalent "old-school" SQL is a bit long.
Notice this SQL standard is poorly supported (by only 3 LinqToDB providers) but many have proprietary variations such as `IS [NOT]` in SQLite or `<=>` in MySql. Tests wi…

title: Fix concurrency issues with hints
body_excerpt:
Fix #3137

Issue was caused by storing hints in query cache so it was shared between context which is not correct as our current hint API is per-connection (new hints API should be query-bound!).
So:
- hints from one connection appeared for same query on other connections
- hints cleanup in execution logic lead to concurrency exceptions as this shared hints collection were enumerated in another thread by sql builder

Fix:
- moves hints population to execution phase where there is no way for hint…

Right now we have a lot of places where we use ADO.NET interfaces only to upcast them later to base classes. 

This PR replaces following ADO.NET interfaces with classes:
- `IDataReader` -> `DbDataReader` (except `WriteToServer*` methods in bulk copy mappings)
- `IDataRecord` -> `DbDataReader`
- `IDbCommand` -> `DbCommand`
- `IDbDataParameter` -> `DbParameter`
- `IDbConnection` -> `DbConnection`
- `IDbTransaction` -> `DbTransaction`
- refactor `IAsyncDbConnection`/`IAsyncDbTransaction` interface…

title: Allow to use private and protected parameterized constructor for mapping
summary: [merged] This PR adds an ability for linq2db to map data to classes with only private or protected constructors. Before this PR only public ones were supported.
body_excerpt:
This PR adds an ability for linq2db to map data to classes with only private or protected constructors. Before this PR only public ones were supported. 

This case is useful when you want to map data to encapsulated rich models, which often have closed constructors.

We can also consider smartly selecting which constructor to use (most suitable based on field names). Right now it will only work for classes with single constructor.

title: WIP Row constructors
summary: [closed] This PR adds support for SQL Row Constructors, which basically are SQL tuples, usually with syntax `ROW (1, 2)` or just `(1, 2)`.
body_excerpt:
This PR adds support for SQL Row Constructors, which basically are SQL tuples, usually with syntax `ROW (1, 2)` or just `(1, 2)`.

### Design 
Row constructors are only server-side, they're always represented by a call to `Sql.Row(1, 2)` and they can't be passed as parameters.

The returned type is `SqlRow<T1, T2>`. 
Tuples with more elements simply nest `SqlRow` in the second argument: e.g. `SqlRow<int, SqlRow<int, int>>`  for a triple.
This may look inefficient but remember this will never be …

body_excerpt:
Related topics: #2452, #3247, #1787, #1883, #1545, #2448?, #1276?

# Query Extensions

LinqToDB contains different mechanisms to extend and customize generated SQL. Query Extensions are designed to extend SQL on clause and statement level such as table, join, query hints, etc.

## Common Hint Extensions

The ```QueryExtensionScope``` enumeration defines a scope where this extension is applied to. 

| Value | Method | Applied to | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| None | Path through meth…

- Expression conversion improvements.

title: PostgreSQL 9.5 UPSERT support
summary: [merged] INSERT ... ON CONFLICT clause (UPSERT) support for InsertOrUpdate, see https://www.postgresql.org/docs/9.5/static/sql-insert.html#SQL-ON-CONFLICT
body_excerpt:
INSERT ... ON CONFLICT clause (UPSERT) support for InsertOrUpdate, see https://www.postgresql.org/docs/9.5/static/sql-insert.html#SQL-ON-CONFLICT

It contains questionable code which overrides and restores table alias. But there is no existing facility to create an alias in INSERT clause and I wanted to make as little changes to the core as possible.

title: Fix #3423 LEAD and LAG default parameter type
body_excerpt:
Fix #3423 
This PR fixes the type of `default` in `LEAD` and `LAG`.

It comes with a unit test that uses string as `default` in addition to existing tests that used `int`.

I've also added more `LEAD` and `LAG` convenience overloads because it's pretty uncommon to specify the null modifier.
Now all these are possible: `Lag(expr)`, `Lag(expr, 3)`, `Lag(expr, 3, "default")`, which mirrors the native SQL function specs.

title: Fix support for DataConnection.GetTable in expressions
Row constructors
body_excerpt:
Fix #2204 
Fix #2657
Fix #2844

This PR adds support for SQL Row Constructors, which basically are SQL tuples, usually with syntax `ROW (1, 2)` or just `(1, 2)`.

### Design 
Row constructors are only server-side, they're always represented by a call to `Sql.Row(1, 2)` and they can't be passed as parameters.

The returned type is `SqlRow<T1, T2>`. 
Tuples with more elements simply nest `SqlRow` in the second argument: e.g. `SqlRow<int, SqlRow<int, int>>`  for a triple.
This may look inefficient …

title: Optimize UPDATE SET queries
summary: [closed] I encountered a blocker while working on my SqlRow branch: I noticed that queries inside UPDATE SET expressions are not visited.
body_excerpt:
I encountered a blocker while working on my SqlRow branch: I noticed that queries inside UPDATE SET expressions are not visited.

I traced that to the fact that `SqlUpdateStatement.WalkQueries` doesn't visit them, which I fixed in this PR.

Notice that I only visit "full query" statements, which is enough to unlock me. In reality an expression could be:
`SET x = 1 + (select 2)`
and I'm not visiting those queries (not sure how it would be done).

⚠ More tests from me showed that there's a problem…

title: Better Contains/In translation when CompareNullsAsValues is false
summary: [merged] This is a rework of #2949 for release 4.0.
body_excerpt:
This is a rework of #2949 for release 4.0.

🚨 **Breaking change for v4.0** 🚨 
It changes the generated SQL of `Contains`, `In`, `NotIn` when `CompareNullsAsValues` is `false`: 
in this case values are not sniffed anymore and a straightforward `[NOT] IN` is generated.

Some examples:
`CompareNullsAsValues == true`, as today, no change(*):
```
a.In(1, null) -> a IN (1) OR a IS NULL
a.NotIn(1, null) -> a NOT IN (1) AND a IS NOT NULL (*)
a.NotIn(1) -> a NOT IN (1) OR a IS NULL
```
(*) This is actu…

title: [WIP] Introducing an AST factory
summary: [closed] This is an attempt at improving the internals of linq2db, there is no change for users.
body_excerpt:
This is an attempt at improving the internals of linq2db, there is no change for users.
As linq2db is large and complex, the changes in the PR are just "getting started" and far from complete, although as I will discuss below it should probably be merged incrementally in multiple steps..

## Main idea: add an AST factory
The core idea of this PR is to introduce a factory class to create our SQL abstract syntax tree.
So, instead of new'ing up nodes with `new SqlPredicate.IsNull(...)` and friends,…

title: Performance: Migrate to Helper methods for exception throwing
body_excerpt:
Why: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/communitytoolkit/developer-tools/throwhelper

TLDR: 
* Prologue for exceptions is usually long; moving to functions allows JIT to reduce code size of functions
* JIT is not allowed to inline any method that includes throwing an exception
* Code around exceptions can be improved when JIT can see that a function call is made only to throw an exception

## comment

why it differs between 
ALL_MVIEWS and  USER_MVIEWS?

it seems that materialized view comments are not in USER_TAB_COMMENTS, so the join kicks out the  USER_MVIEWS

but
materialized view seem to bee in ALL_TAB_COMMENTS, so this statement is simpler.

title: Improve log when db type can not be determined
summary: [merged] Helps to figure out which column is the issue when creating table with type that can not be determined.
body_excerpt:
Helps to figure out which column is the issue when creating table with type that can not be determined.

Log was:
> Unhandled exception. LinqToDB.LinqToDBException: Database type cannot be determined automatically and must be specified explicitly

Now (diff in bold):
> Unhandled exception. LinqToDB.LinqToDBException: Database **column** type cannot be determined automatically and must be specified explicitly **for system type NodaTime.Instant**

Occurs when .net 7 is picked up rather than .net 6.

Error fixed:

```
dotnet linq2db scaffold -i Portal.json -c "Server=localhost;Database=Portal;TrustServerCertificate=true;Integrated Security=SSPI" --customize Generic.tt
AssemblyResolve: Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.resources, Version=4.4.0.0, Culture=en-GB, PublicKe…

title: Refactor work with attributes
body_excerpt:
Main goal of this PR:
- separate mapping attributes and non-mapping attributes. Mapping attributes are attributes resolved through mapping schema, non-mapping attributes resolved through reflection
- optimize work with attributes to better use caching. Currently caching support is very limited
- cleanup and improve related API surface to remove not needed/not used functionality and fix usability issues with API

Changes:
- moved `string? Configuration` property to base `MappingAttribute` class. …

title: Support C# nullable references
body_excerpt:
Fixes #2690

When there is no explicit `CanBeNull` annotation, i.e. `[Column]` has not set `CanBeNull` and there is no `[Nullable]`, before falling back to default rules, we use the C# nullable reference type info if it is available.

Same for associations, although it should be noted that `[Association]` is used both for many-to-one "associations" and many-to-many "collections". Nullability is inferred for true "associations" (single value), but never for "collections" as the fact that there is…

title: Refactor BasicSqlBuilder.DataProvider
summary: [closed] - Make it non-nullable (it is never null)
body_excerpt:
- Make it non-nullable (it is never null)
- Make it generic to get rid of all the casts in derived classes

PostgreSqlBulkCopy could get rid of its casts if `CreateSqlBuilder` used co-variance, but our old .net targets don't allow that.
BasicSqlBuilder could also use the curiously recursive pattern but I feel like it's not worth doing here.

title: Better nulls handling in Contains
summary: [closed] 1. LinqToDb attempts to sniff `null` constants in `Sql.In` arguments and include them in a comparison (with `x IN (1,2) or x IS NULL` and `x NOT IN (1, 2) and x IS NOT NULL`).
body_excerpt:
1. LinqToDb attempts to sniff `null` constants in `Sql.In` arguments and include them in a comparison (with `x IN (1,2) or x IS NULL` and `x NOT IN (1, 2) and x IS NOT NULL`). 
This is a non-standard SQL behavior that really should have been behind `CompareNullsAsValues`, but it is not. To not break backwards compatiblity, I created a new configuration to disable it: `CheckNullsInContains`.
The case `null not in (null)` can become just too confusing, so implicitly if `CompareNullsAsValues` is tr…

title: Error on untyped enum values in Postgres

It does not fix #4046. We are not going to change the default behavior as this is too general. However, we add an option for a workaround.

```c#
MappingSchema.Default.SetConverter<object?,DataParameter>(
    o => new(null, o is Enum e ? MappingSchema.Default.EnumToValue(e) : o, DataType.Undefined));
```

title: BUG: Using hint in subquery.
body_excerpt:
The following code

```c#
from c in db.Child
select new
{
	cc =
	(
		from c1 in db.Child
		.AsSqlServer().WithNoLock()
		where c1.ParentID == c.ParentID select c1
	).Count()
}
```

generates an exception

```
value(Tests.Model.TestDataConnection).GetTable().AsSqlServer().TableHint("NoLock").Where(c1 => (c1.ParentID == c.ParentID)).Count()'
cannot be converted to SQL.
```

title: BUG: GroupBy with subquery.
body_excerpt:
The following code 

```c#
from pmp in
(
	from pmp  in db.Child
	group pmp by pmp.ParentID into g
	select g.Key
)
from pmp1 in db.Child
select new { pmp1.ChildID }
```

generates wrong SQL

```sql
SELECT
	[pmp1].[ChildID]
FROM
	[Child] [pmp],
	[Child] [pmp1]
GROUP BY
	[pmp].[ParentID]
```

should be 

```sql
SELECT
	[pmp1].[ChildID]
FROM
	(
		SELECT
			[t1].[ParentID]
		FROM
			[Child] [t1]
		GROUP BY
			[t1].[ParentID]
	) [pmp],
	[Child] [pmp1]
```

title: BUG: Conditional with null
body_excerpt:
```c#
from p in db.Parent
from i in new[] { 0, 1 }
select new
{
	ID    = i == 0 ? null : (int?)p.ParentID,
	Value = p.Value1
} into p
where p.ID == p.Value // p.ID is considered as NOT nullable
select p
```

I've used following model to test:

Tested models: 150 entities and 1500 entities (to detect bottlenecks, not visible enough on smaller model)

title: Partial fix for transaction isolation level for multi-statement eager load sql server queries
body_excerpt:
Fix #2790

### Problem:

When user execute eager load query which requires multiple queries to load data and there is no transaction available, linq2db will automatically wrap all queries into transaction with predefined isolation level (see below).
Due to `SqlClient` behavior where it doesn't reset current transaction isolation level even after connection was returned to connection pool, it changes transaction isolation level for pooled connection.
It is not a problem, when user always use expl…

title: Improve sequence building
summary: [merged] This is a #3913 redone on latest v6 master branch.
body_excerpt:
This is a #3913 redone on latest v6 master branch.
I'm gonna copy-paste my original description below:

----

### Limitations of current design
Today `ExpressionBuilder` linearly goes through a long list (~70) of `ISequenceBuilder` to find which builder can handle an `Expression`. Each builder is probed by a call to `CanBuild`, in sequence. 

**This is horribly not performant.** If your query uses one of the last builders -- or simply the expression has no builder, which is sometimes a normal ca…

title: Improve sequence building performance
summary: [closed] Today `ExpressionBuilder` linearly goes through a long list (~70) of `ISequenceBuilder` to find which builder can handle an `Expression`. Each builder is probed by a call to `CanBuild`, in sequence.
body_excerpt:
### Limitations of current design
Today `ExpressionBuilder` linearly goes through a long list (~70) of `ISequenceBuilder` to find which builder can handle an `Expression`. Each builder is probed by a call to `CanBuild`, in sequence. 

**This is horribly not performant.** If your query uses one of the last builders -- or simply the expression has no builder, which is sometimes a normal case -- you have to go through 70+ checks!! They are virtual calls, many builders repeatedly perform the same ca…

body_excerpt:
Move SQL generation from `ToString` overload on query to separate API as:
- use of `ToString` makes this heavy operation being called a lot by IDE debugger visualizer
- `ToString` generates single SQL string with all commands and parameters in `SQLServer`-like syntax making it problematic to use for SQL generation

New implementation:
- returns SQL command and command parameters
- don't generate unnecessary SQL comments (comments not supported by Acccess)
- adds missing support for `Merge` and `…

title: Add DataContextModifier option to ScaffoldCommand
body_excerpt:
Hey

In my ongoing quest to get my scaffolding exactly like I want it, I now have the need for changing the access modifier of the generated Context class.

I've added a property to the scaffolding dataModel that enables this.
When not specified it does as before.

I'm not entirely sure where or if it would be appropriate to add this new option to tests.

  - SqlJson type with unknown purpose as does…

Note that the tests still pass; I'm still trying to diagnose the issue in my application and determine if it's a linq2db regression or an issue in my application.

title: Scaffolding - add entity type discriminator to fluent mapping
summary: [merged] Introduce a new option, FluentEntityTypeDiscriminators, to the ScaffoldCommand configuration, which changes how the fluent mapping is built.
body_excerpt:
Introduce a new option, FluentEntityTypeDiscriminators, to the ScaffoldCommand configuration, which changes how the fluent mapping is built.
When not specified fluent mapping is built like it currently is.

It's enabled by providing one or more method names which will be called immediately after Entity<>.
These methods must either be present on the EntityMappingBuilder<T> or more likely as extension methods to it.

title: TempTable.Insert method is broken.
ORDER BY is removed from subquery even when TOP or LIMIT/OFFSET are used
body_excerpt:
See test.

`group by` itself has `order by`.

```sql
DECLARE @take Int -- Int32
SET     @take = 2

/* GroupBy */
SELECT TOP (@take)
	[t1].[Key_1],
	[t1].[Max_1]
FROM
	(
		SELECT
			MAX([g_1].[ChildID]) as [Max_1],
			[g_1].[ParentID] as [Key_1]
		FROM
			[Child] [g_1]
		GROUP BY
			[g_1].[ParentID]
	) [t1]
ORDER BY
	[t1].[Max_1] DESC
```

If it is used as subquery `order by` is lost.

```sql
/* GroupBy
Query */
SELECT
	[t].[ParentID],
	[t].[ChildID]
FROM
	[Child] [t]
WHERE
	[t].[ParentID] IN (
	…

title: Remove ISqlBuilder
summary: [closed] Implementing `ISqlBuilder` is a extremely complex, as it should be able to convert any AST to SQL string.
body_excerpt:
Implementing `ISqlBuilder` is a extremely complex, as it should be able to convert any AST to SQL string.
`BasicSqlBuilder` is the only class that implements this interface and it's pretty certain that there will never be another one.
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ab5947ef-1eaa-4dd2-87d7-09237bdfddea)

I have removed the interface and renamed `BasicSqlBuilder` to simply `SqlBuilder`.

The Client project includes the the `HttpDataContext` context, its registration and configuration.
The Server project includes the LinqToDB API Controller.
The interaction between the client and the server is implemented using `HttpClient`.

Unfortunately, the projects cannot be merged, because they must …

title: Unbanned some integer apis
summary: [merged] For integers, I removed from banned api list:
body_excerpt:
For integers, I removed from banned api list:
- `.ToString` without format string
- `.Parse` and `.TryParse` without `NumberStyles`
- `StringBuilder.Append` and `StringBuilder.Insert`

Without a format or `NumberStyles`, .net formats integers without thousand separator, so culture doesn't matter.

I have updated a few trivial places `Append(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, $"{value}")` as it's shorter code and it avoids boxing / allocations (at least on older .NET releases).

title: Remove IsTakeSupported from SqlProviderFlags
summary: [merged] `IsTakeSupported` was probably a legacy provider flags that was used before.
body_excerpt:
`IsTakeSupported` was probably a legacy provider flags that was used before.
Currently all providers have `IsTakeSupported = true`, even the few that don't support TOP/TAKE/FETCH such as Oracle 11.

I'm noting that we have 60 flags remaining, that's a lot of bool / bytes. It would fit into just 8 bytes (a `long`) if we stored them as a bitfield. That would also simplify serialization (numbering 60 `Datamember` is no fun) as well as `Equals` and `GetHashCode` implementations.

In fact `GetHashCod…

Currently, the reflection code in `LinqToDBForEFToolsImplDefault.GetCurrentContext` fails if the compiler returned from `IQueryable._queryCompiler` is a subclass of `QueryCompiler`.

This is e.g. the case when using [EntityFrameworkCore.Projectables](https://github.com/koenbeuk/EntityFrameworkCore.Projectables).

I'm too inexperienced with EF Core's internals to tell whether this is actually a valid use case or not, but it was a simple fix in the implementation, so I thought I'd propose this her…

title: Fixed IsNotNull method misleading summary (Issue #4941)
summary: [closed] The summary tag for the IsNotNull() method was previously duplicating the description
body_excerpt:
The summary tag for the IsNotNull() method was previously duplicating the description 
used by IsNullable(), which was misleading. This change updates the documentation 
to clearly state that IsNotNull() marks the column as NOT NULL, disallowing any NULL values.

See the [issue](https://github.com/linq2db/linq2db/issues/4941)for details

title: Left Join issue
summary: [merged] The following code is not working:
body_excerpt:
The following code is not working:

```c#
from p in db.Parent
join c in db.Child on p.Value1 equals c.ParentID into g
from c in g.DefaultIfEmpty()
where g == null
select p.ParentID;
```

```
LinqToDB.LinqToDBException : The LINQ expression 'g' could not be converted to SQL.
```


Worked in 5.4.1.

summary: [merged] The following code is not working
body_excerpt:
The following code is not working 

```c#
from t2 in temp1
join t3 in temp2 on t2.ID equals t3.ID into gt3
from t3 in gt3.DefaultIfEmpty()
let Value = t3.Value ?? t2.Value
//let Value = Sql.AsNullable(t3.Value) ?? t2.Value
join t4 in temp3 on new { Value } equals new { t4.Value } into gt5
from t4 in gt5.DefaultIfEmpty()
select t4
```

If you uncomment the commented line, the code works.

Worked in 2.xxx

title: UseOptions / UseMappingSchema
summary: [merged] A new method, `UseOptions`, has been introduced to allow temporary changes to `IDataContext` options, with automatic restoration afterward.
body_excerpt:
A new method, `UseOptions`, has been introduced to allow temporary changes to `IDataContext` options, with automatic restoration afterward.

`IDataContext` options are immutable, meaning they cannot be changed once set. With this feature, it is now possible to override options within a specific execution context while ensuring they revert to their original state when no longer needed.

Several overloads are available to fit different use cases:

```c#
using (db.UseOptions(o => o
	.WithOptions<Li…

title: Feature. LEFT JOIN transformation
body_excerpt:
Fix #5004

A `LEFT JOIN` can be converted to an `INNER JOIN` when the `WHERE` clause enforces a condition that guarantees non-null values from the joined table - such as checking for `NOT NULL` on a non-nullable column or comparing it to a constant.

Examples:

```sql
SELECT
    *
FROM Some
LEFT JOIN Joined j ON ...
WHERE j.Id IS NOT NULL
```

```sql
SELECT
    *
FROM Some
LEFT JOIN Joined j ON ...
WHERE j.Name = 'John'
```
Also it is possible to transform LEFT JOIN if it is followew by INNER JO…

title: Disable mapping of  ValueType's to scalar types by default
body_excerpt:
Related to #5056

- Fix #5056

New parser use information about scalar types better than old one, leading to situations where incorrect type attribution to scalar leads to errors.

To solve it we should stop treatening structs as scalars by default and require user to mark scalar types explicitly for structs too. This doesn't affect well-known scalar types and provider-specific types as they are registered as scalars in mapping schema already (with some fixes in this PR)

**Add tests exposing invalid `STRING_AGG` SQL generation in Update with child collection**

This PR does not fix the issue; it only adds tests that reproduce it.

For the following query:

```c#
var query =
	from t in table
	select new
	{
		t,

	});

title: Added .NET 6.0+ Hot Reload compatibility
summary: [open] Due to the heavy use of Reflection in static (global) variables in linq2db, running queries after a Hot Reload may issue exceptions due to mismatch metadata info. This pull request re-reads and updates reflection data in such variables every time a hot reload is triggered, avoidi...
body_excerpt:
Due to the heavy use of Reflection in static (global) variables in linq2db, running queries after a Hot Reload may issue exceptions due to mismatch metadata info. This pull request re-reads and updates reflection data in such variables every time a hot reload is triggered, avoiding such exceptions.

More info: https://github.com/dotnet/sdk/issues/23736

These only allow defining setters one-by-one, so I'm adding some new overloads to make it more convenient to use.

title: IN SubQuery with Distinct optimization.
body_excerpt:
The following query

```c#
from p in db.Parent
where p.ParentID.In(db.Child.Select(c => c.ParentID).Distinct())
select p;
```

generates SQL:

```sql
SELECT
	[p].[ParentID],
	[p].[Value1]
FROM
	[Parent] [p]
WHERE
	[p].[ParentID] IN (
		SELECT
			[t1].[In_1]
		FROM
			(
				SELECT DISTINCT
					[c_1].[ParentID] as [In_1]
				FROM
					[Child] [c_1]
			) [t1]
	)
```

title: Implicit conversion bug
summary: [draft] Add regression test for incorrect `NULL` handling when mapping via implicit conversion operators.
body_excerpt:
Add regression test for incorrect `NULL` handling when mapping via implicit conversion operators. 

When a database column value is `NULL`, the mapper currently assigns `null` to the destination wrapper object itself, instead of creating the wrapper instance and passing `null` into it (i.e., `new ImplicitValue<T> { Value = null })`. 

This breaks wrapper semantics and produces different results compared to the same mapping performed in memory.

title: Fix operator precedence bug in translator list initial capacity
summary: [draft] `??` has lower precedence than `+`, so `o.UnaryTranslators?.Count ?? 0 + 1` parses as `o.UnaryTranslators?.Count ?? 1` instead of `(o.UnaryTranslators?.Count ?? 0) + 1`.
body_excerpt:
`??` has lower precedence than `+`, so `o.UnaryTranslators?.Count ?? 0 + 1` parses as `o.UnaryTranslators?.Count ?? 1` instead of `(o.UnaryTranslators?.Count ?? 0) + 1`.

### Changes
- Added parentheses to ensure correct capacity calculation in:
  - `UseUnaryTranslator`
  - `UseMemberTranslator`
  - `UseBinaryTranslator`

```csharp
// Before (bug): capacity = Count ?? 1
var list = new List<IUnaryTranslator>(o.UnaryTranslators?.Count ?? 0 + 1);

// After (fixed): capacity = (Count ?? 0) + 1
var l…

title: Fix CTE columns incorrectly removed by optimizer
summary: [merged] - Don't remove columns from CTE body queries in `SqlQueryColumnOptimizerVisitor` — CTE column structure must be preserved for WITH clause header, UNION ALL alignment, and field-to-column index mapping
body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Don't remove columns from CTE body queries in `SqlQueryColumnOptimizerVisitor` — CTE column structure must be preserved for WITH clause header, UNION ALL alignment, and field-to-column index mapping
- Simplified `SynchronizeCteFields` to keep fields based solely on column existence, removing the `_usedCteFields` double-filter that could desynchronize fields from body columns
- Added regression test `RecursiveHierarchyWithJoinOnCteColumn`

Fixes #5457

title: Fix WHERE clause lost during nested UNION ALL flattening
body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Fixes #5447
- `OptimizeUnions`: check `HasWhere`/`HasGroupBy`/`HasModifier` before flattening nested set operators, even when the outer query already has set operators
- `IsMovingUpValid`: move `HasWhere`/`HasGroupBy`/`HasHaving`/`HasModifier`/`HasOrderBy` guard before `HasSetOperators` early return so it is always evaluated

title: Fix null checks optimization for parameterized predicates (#5445)
summary: [merged] - Fix `SimilarityMerger.TryMerge` incorrectly merging `IS NOT NULL` guard with equality predicate when the checked expression contains a query parameter
body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Fix `SimilarityMerger.TryMerge` incorrectly merging `IS NOT NULL` guard with equality predicate when the checked expression contains a query parameter
- When `param.HasValue && column == param` was used with a null parameter, the optimizer stripped the `HasValue` check, producing `column IS NULL` instead of `1 = 0`
- Added `HasQueryParameter()` guard to skip the merge when the `IS NOT NULL` target contains a parameter

Fixes #5445

title: Fix UnionAll + string member access (.Length, IsNullOrEmpty)
summary: [merged] Fixes #5458 — `string.IsNullOrEmpty()` and `.Length` on UnionAll columns produced wrong SQL (missing `LENGTH()` call, comparing column value to `0` instead).
body_excerpt:
## Summary

Fixes #5458 — `string.IsNullOrEmpty()` and `.Length` on UnionAll columns produced wrong SQL (missing `LENGTH()` call, comparing column value to `0` instead).

### Root cause

`string.IsNullOrEmpty(x)` expands to `x == null || x.Length == 0`. On UnionAll columns, `.Length` couldn't be resolved through projection (terminal `SqlPlaceholderExpression`/`SqlPathExpression`), and the fallback to member translation (`LENGTH()`) was not triggered.

### Changes

- **ExpressionBuilder.SqlBuilde…

title: Fix PostgreSQL array parameter query cache misses
body_excerpt:
Fixes #5302

Array parameters in `Sql.Extension` methods (e.g. `ValueIsEqualToAny`) caused query cache misses on every execution because arrays were compared by reference in the cache key comparison.

Register primitive array types as scalar in `PostgreSQLMappingSchema` so they are treated as normal parameters instead of going through `MarkAsValue` path.

title: Oracle: use NVL for 2 arguments COALESCE
summary: [merged] Main change in this PR is using proprietary `NVL` in Oracle when `COALESCE` only has 2 arguments.
body_excerpt:
Main change in this PR is using proprietary `NVL` in Oracle when `COALESCE` only has 2 arguments.
It's more concise but also it does not fail because of inconsistent charset, which I encountered as a regression when upgrading to v6.

For 3+ arguments I stick to `COALESCE` because it's just cleaner than `NVL(a, NVL(b, NVL(c)))` especially when involved expressions are large. If linq2db does not detect the charset inconsistency then unfortunately it means user has to handle it.

Idea is to forbid using a parameter or literal that would need a conversion in a ROW assignment, like `row(a, b) = row(12, localVariable)` if `a` or `b` are columns with value converters. This is because converters are not applied properly inside `row` expressions.

Adds an `AsQueryable` overload that gives per-column control over how an `IEnumerable<T>` is rendered into the resulting `VALUES (...)` clause.

```csharp
data.AsQueryable(db, b => b.Parameterize())
data.AsQueryable(db, b => b.Inline())
data.AsQueryable(db, b => b.Parameterize().Except(p => p.Id))
data.AsQueryable(db, b => b.Inline().Except(p => p.Address.Zip))
```

title: Translate DateTimeOffset.DateTime as a cast
body_excerpt:
Fixes #5435 

It looks like linq2db has no conversion for `DateTimeOffset::DateTime` property.
So I don't know how the query in issue was working before v6... 

For reference, in .NET `DateTimeOffset::DateTime` returns the wall-clock date and time of `DateTimeOffset` and simply strips the offset. For example, `2026-04-12 14:00 +07:00` becomes simply `2026-04-12 14:00` regardless of your TZ. The resulting `DateTime` object has `Kind.Unspecified`.

I have added a translation of `DateTimeOffset::Da…

title: Improve "now" translation to support TZ and UTC variations
body_excerpt:
Fixes #5436 

In RDBMS that support timestamps with time zone, and actually store the offset, we need 4 different translations to capture the nuances of `Now` and `UtcNow` in both `DateTime` and `DateTimeOffset` variations.

Most RDBMS don't support time zones so that doesn't change much in their provider code.
For compatibility with current releases, in those providers `DateTimeOffset.Now | UtcNow` do not throw an error but are "downgraded" into `DateTime.Now | UtcNow` expressions.

I improved …

title: Fix #5404: wrap non-nullable subquery aggregates with COALESCE
summary: [open] In v6, `Sum`/`Min`/`Max`/`Average` are inlined into SQL when used inside a subquery projection (e.g. `select MaxWeight - sub.Sum(...)`). When the SQL aggregate returns NULL on empty input, the outer arithmetic goes NULL — breaking queries whose C# return type is non-nullable. The...
body_excerpt:
## Summary

In v6, `Sum`/`Min`/`Max`/`Average` are inlined into SQL when used inside a subquery projection (e.g. `select MaxWeight - sub.Sum(...)`). When the SQL aggregate returns NULL on empty input, the outer arithmetic goes NULL — breaking queries whose C# return type is non-nullable. The existing runtime `CheckNullValue` validator can't fire when the aggregate result is consumed by SQL itself rather than materialized directly.

`Sum` of an empty set is defined as `0` by LINQ. The fix wraps n…

title: Fix C# string concatenation null semantics
summary: [closed] Fixes C# string concatenation null semantics for translated LINQ expressions.
body_excerpt:
**Summary**

Fixes C# string concatenation null semantics for translated LINQ expressions.

`string + string` now treats nullable operands like C# does: `null` is concatenated as an empty string instead of letting SQL null-propagation turn the whole result into `NULL`. The translation only adds `COALESCE(..., '')` for operands that can actually be nullable, avoiding unnecessary SQL for non-null operands.

**Changes**
